meta.disclaimer: Planning prototype only. Not legal, immigration, tax, medical, financial, or school-placement advice. Verify with qualified professionals and current official sources.

plan.title: The Big Trip
plan.household_summary: Nate, his wife, and their three-year-old child are exploring a possible Barcelona move.
plan.collaborator_summary: Nate's wife reviews the plan during family planning conversations.

roadmap_phases.description: Compare neighborhoods through family stability needs.

milestones.notes: Initial planning docs identify core unknowns and official sources.

milestones.notes: Define what stability means for the household.

milestones.title: First safe budget range
milestones.notes: Use ranges until current costs are researched.

milestones.title: Professional visa/tax consult
milestones.notes: Professional advice required before treating any path as viable.

milestones.title: Document timing calendar
milestones.notes: Focus on documents with issue/expiration windows.

milestones.notes: Compare family fit, rent, schools, transit, healthcare, parks, noise, and padrón feasibility.

milestones.notes: Treat school placement as a planning question, not a current decision.

milestones.notes: Revisit decisions after real arrival conditions are known.

budget_items.notes: Needs current market research and family location preferences.

budget_items.notes: Must be validated against selected visa requirements.

budget_items.notes: Family safety buffer; amount requires household-specific planning.

decisions.title: Which residence path should we investigate first?
decisions.rationale: Visa fit controls timeline, documents, budget, healthcare, and work assumptions.
decisions.notes: Requires professional immigration/tax review before deciding.

decisions.title: What monthly budget range feels safe?
decisions.rationale: Budget comfort affects neighborhood, school, temporary lodging, and timing choices.
decisions.notes: Use ranges and confidence labels until current estimates are gathered.

decisions.title: Which neighborhood factors matter most for our family?
decisions.rationale: The family needs healthcare, school access, parks, transit, and housing stability.
decisions.notes: Neighborhood examples are placeholders for comparison structure.

decisions.title: What school or childcare environment should we prioritize?
decisions.rationale: Child transition, language environment, and school timing are central family concerns.

options.name: Telework / digital nomad path
options.summary: Potential long-stay path for remote work tied to non-Spanish companies.
pros: Could match remote work if requirements fit
cons: Tax and Social Security implications are uncertain

options.name: Non-lucrative residence path
options.summary: Residence without work if sufficient means are proven.
cons: Official LA source says it does not allow teleworking
options.notes: Only viable if household work/income plans align.

options.name: Short exploratory trip
options.summary: Use a short stay for research, school visits, neighborhoods, and appointments.
pros: Can improve confidence in neighborhood and school assumptions
options.notes: Planning option, not a residence path.

options.name: Gracia family-fit placeholder
options.summary: Placeholder neighborhood comparison card focused on walkability and family amenities.
cons: Rent, noise, and exact school access need research
options.notes: Generic placeholder until neighborhood research is current.

options.name: Sarria / Sant Gervasi quiet-fit placeholder
options.summary: Placeholder for quieter family stability and school comparisons.
cons: Transit and rent need current research

options.name: Poblenou transit/coast placeholder
options.summary: Placeholder for comparing transit, parks, beach access, and housing fit.
pros: Could be useful for family outdoor time
cons: Housing costs and school catchment need research

options.name: Public second-cycle preschool
options.summary: Barcelona source describes ages 3-6 second cycle, generally in primary schools.
pros: Public second-cycle described as free and universal
cons: Meals, supplies, timing, language, and mid-year arrival questions remain
options.notes: Enrollment requirements must be checked near move timing.

options.name: Private or international preschool
options.summary: Alternative if timing, language, or availability makes public path difficult.
pros: May offer language fit or easier transition
Potential backup if public placement uncertain

ideas.prompt: What would make Barcelona feel stable for our child in the first three months?
What monthly cost range would let us plan without feeling stretched?
Which work/income assumptions need professional review before visa planning?
ideas.notes: Do not resolve without qualified advice.

ideas.prompt: Would we trade a smaller apartment for better walkability, school access, or parks?
How much Spanish or Catalan prep should become part of weekly family routine?

tasks.title: Discuss family priorities for the Barcelona move
tasks.conversation_prompt: What would make Barcelona feel stable for our child in the first three months?
tasks.notes: Use a calm family conversation to name what matters most: stability, childcare, healthcare, housing, walkability, budget comfort, and arrival routines.

tasks.title: List family must-haves for housing and daily life
tasks.conversation_prompt: Which housing and daily-life filters are non-negotiable for our family?
tasks.notes: Conversation follow-up that turns priorities into filters.

tasks.title: Write income/work assumptions for visa consult
tasks.notes: Keep generic in app; no account details.

tasks.title: Compare telework vs non-lucrative assumptions
tasks.notes: Conversation output should narrow the decision.

tasks.title: Research current cost ranges for first budget pass
Decide family emergency buffer target
tasks.notes: Turn researched ranges into a comfort level.

tasks.title: Turn neighborhood shortlist into housing filters
tasks.notes: Subtask should follow the shortlist conversation.

tasks.title: Map child age and arrival timing to preschool questions
tasks.notes: Use official source and keep questions open.

tasks.title: Write school and childcare question list
tasks.notes: Conversation output should be easy to share.

tasks.title: List insurance questions for each visa option
Create document inventory and timing watch
Turn document inventory into deadline calendar
tasks.notes: Subtask for timing windows and ordering.

tasks.title: Assemble arrival packet and first-week routines
Choose a gentle family language prep routine

risks.title: Visa path may not match work/income model
risks.description: A path that looks promising may not fit household work or income details.
risks.trigger: Professional review finds mismatch or added requirement.
risks.mitigation: Write assumptions clearly and consult qualified immigration/tax professionals before deciding.
risks.notes: Do not present visa options as advice.

risks.title: Budget range may be too optimistic
risks.description: Housing, insurance, school, travel, or document costs may exceed placeholders.
risks.mitigation: Use low/high ranges, source dates, and an emergency buffer.
risks.notes: Budget view should show uncertainty visibly.

risks.title: Housing may not support padrón timing
risks.description: A temporary or informal arrangement could complicate registration and downstream steps.
risks.trigger: Housing option lacks clear registration path.
risks.mitigation: Track padrón feasibility as a first-class housing comparison factor.

risks.title: Mid-year child transition may be difficult
risks.description: Arrival timing, language, and placement may affect family stability.
risks.trigger: School options or timing are unclear by late 2026.
risks.mitigation: List school questions early and keep backup options visible.

risks.title: Insurance may not satisfy visa or transition needs
risks.description: Insurance requirements can vary by visa path and timing.
risks.trigger: Selected visa path has specific insurance requirements.
risks.mitigation: Review insurance requirements with qualified sources before purchase.

risks.title: Documents may have strict issue windows
risks.description: Background checks, apostilles, translations, and certificates may need careful timing.
risks.trigger: Requirement says document must be recent or officially translated.
risks.mitigation: Create a timing calendar before ordering time-sensitive documents.

risks.title: Arrival month could become overwhelming
risks.description: Housing, registration, healthcare, school, phones, and routines may collide.
risks.trigger: Too many tasks are left until arrival month.
risks.mitigation: Create a first-week checklist and print local snapshot before travel.

documents.name: Passports for each family member
documents.notes: Track validity only; do not store passport numbers.

documents.name: Adult background check placeholder
documents.notes: Confirm requirement and timing with selected path.

documents.name: Medical and vaccination records
documents.notes: Do not store medical details in app.

documents.name: School/childcare records placeholder
documents.notes: Confirm exactly what is needed before collecting.

documents.name: Housing contract or registration authorization
documents.notes: Padrón feasibility should be checked before committing.

documents.name: TSI healthcare card
documents.notes: Eligibility depends on status and registration steps.
